help.describe: Show help for a command or subcommand

api-key.describe: Neon API key, authenticates without `neon auth`

context-file.describe: Context file with default org, project, and branch IDs, created by `neon link`
context-file.defaultText: nearest .neon file, searching upward from the current directory

org-id.describe: List projects of a given organization

recoverable-only.describe: List only deleted projects within their deletion grace period

block-public-connections.describe:
When set, connections from the public internet
are disallowed. This supersedes the AllowedIPs list.
This parameter is under active development and its semantics may change in the future.

block-vpc-connections.describe:
When set, connections using VPC endpoints are disallowed.
This parameter is under active development and its semantics may change in the future.

hipaa.describe: Enable HIPAA compliance for the project.

name.describe: The project name. If not specified, the name will be identical to the generated project ID

region-id.describe: The region ID. Possible values: aws-us-west-2, aws-ap-southeast-1, aws-ap-southeast-2, aws-eu-central-1, aws-us-east-2, aws-us-east-1, azure-eastus2

psql.describe: Connect to a new project via psql

fallback.describe: Force the embedded TypeScript psql fallback (for testing)

database.describe: The database name. If not specified, the default database name, `neondb`, will be used.

role.describe: The role name. If not specified, the default role name, `{database_name}_owner`, will be used.

set-context.describe: Set the current context to the new project

cu.describe: The number of Compute Units. Could be a fixed size (e.g. "2") or a range delimited by a dash (e.g. "0.5-3").

block-vpc-connections.describe:
When set, connections using VPC endpoints are disallowed.
This parameter is under active development and its semantics may change in the future.
 Use --block-vpc-connections=false to set the value to false.

block-public-connections.describe:
When set, connections from the public internet
are disallowed. This supersedes the AllowedIPs list.
This parameter is under active development and its semantics may change in the future.
 Use --block-public-connections=false to set the value to false.

hipaa.describe: Enable HIPAA compliance for the project.

cu.describe: The number of Compute Units. Could be a fixed size (e.g. "2") or a range delimited by a dash (e.g. "0.5-3").

recover.name: recover
recover.describe: Recovers a deleted project during the deletion grace period

positionals.name: ips
positionals.describe: The list of IP addresses to add

protected-only.describe: If true, the list will be applied only to protected branches.

add.describe: Add IP addresses to the IP allowlist

remove.name: remove

positionals.name: ips
positionals.describe: The list of IP addresses to remove

remove.describe: Remove IP addresses from the IP allowlist

positionals.name: ips
positionals.describe: The list of IP addresses to reset

endpoint.name: endpoint

region-id.describe: The region ID. Possible values: aws-us-west-2, aws-ap-southeast-1, aws-ap-southeast-2, aws-eu-central-1, aws-us-east-2, aws-us-east-1, azure-eastus2

list.name: list
list.describe: List configured VPC endpoints for this organization.

assign.name: assign

label.describe: An optional descriptive label for the VPC endpoint

assign.describe:
Add or update a VPC endpoint for this organization.
Note: Azure regions are not yet supported.

remove.name: remove
remove.describe: Remove a VPC endpoint from this organization.

status.name: status
status.describe: Get the status of a VPC endpoint for this organization.

endpoint.describe:
Manage VPC endpoints.
See: https://neon.tech/docs/guides/neon-private-networking
After adding an endpoint to an organization, client connections will be accepted
from the corresponding VPC for all projects in the organization, unless overridden
by a project-level VPC endpoint restriction.

project.name: project

list.name: list
list.describe: List VPC endpoint restrictions for this project.

restrict.name: restrict

label.describe: An optional descriptive label for the VPC endpoint restriction

restrict.describe: Configure or update a VPC endpoint restriction for this project.

remove.name: remove
remove.describe: Remove a VPC endpoint restriction from this project.

project.describe:
Manage project-level VPC endpoint restrictions.
By default, connections are accepted from any VPC configured at the organization level.
A project-level VPC endpoint restriction can be used to restrict connections to a specific VPC.

vpc.describe: Manage VPC endpoints and project VPC restrictions

parent.describe: Parent branch name or id or timestamp or LSN. Defaults to the default branch

compute.describe: Create a branch with or without a compute. By default branch is created with a read-write compute. To create a branch without compute use --no-compute

suspend-timeout.describe:
Duration of inactivity in seconds after which the compute endpoint is
automatically suspended. The value `0` means use the global default.
The value `-1` means never suspend. The default value is `300` seconds (5 minutes).
The maximum value is `604800` seconds (1 week).

cu.describe: The number of Compute Units. Could be a fixed size (e.g. "2") or a range delimited by a dash (e.g. "0.5-3").

psql.describe: Connect to a new branch via psql

fallback.describe: Force the embedded TypeScript psql fallback (for testing)

schema-only.describe: Create a schema-only branch. Requires exactly one read-write compute.

expires-at.describe: Set an expiration date for the branch. Accepts a date string (e.g., 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z).

preserve-under-name.describe: Name under which to preserve the old branch

restore.name: restore

preserve-under-name.describe: Name under which to preserve the old branch

restore.describe:
Restores a branch to a specific point in time
<source> can be: ^self, ^parent, or <source-branch-id|name>

examples.description: Restore my-branch to the head of its parent branch
Restores my-branch to the LSN 0/123456 from its own history
Restores main to the timestamp 2021-01-01T00:00:00Z of the source branch
Restores main to the head of the branch with id br-source-branch-123456

set-expiration.name: set-expiration

expires-at.describe: Set a expiration date for the branch. If omitted, expiration will be removed. Format [RFC3339]: 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z

set-expiration.describe: Set an expiration date for the branch

schema-diff.name: schema-diff

database.describe: Name of the database for which the schema comparison is performed

schema-diff.describe: Compare the latest schemas of any two branches, or compare a branch to its own or another branch's history.

examples.description: If a branch is specified in 'set-context', compares this branch to its parent. Otherwise, compares the default branch to its parent.
Compares my-branch to the head of its parent branch
Compares my-branch to LSN 0/123456 from its own history
Compares the main branch to the state of the compare branch at timestamp 2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z
Compares the main branch to the head of the branch with ID br-compare-branch-123456

set-context.describe: Deprecated: use `neonctl link`. Set the .neon context (raw write).

positionals.name: id|name
positionals.describe: Branch name or id to check out. Omit to pick interactively from the list of branches.

env-pull.describe: Pull the branch's Neon env vars (DATABASE_URL, ...) into a local .env after checkout. On by default; use --no-env-pull to skip, for example when injecting env at runtime with `neon-env run` (from `@neon/env`) or `neon dev`.

checkout.describe: Pin a branch in the local context (.neon) so subsequent commands target it

examples.description: Pin a branch by id for an explicit project
Pin the branch named "main" in the closest .neon file
Pick a branch interactively from the project in the closest .neon file

project-id.describe: Existing project ID to link to

project-name.describe: Name for a new project to create and link to

region-id.describe: Region ID for a new project (e.g. aws-us-east-2). Required with --project-name.

branch.describe: Branch name or ID to pin in the context (resolved to its ID before writing). Without it, link only resolves the org and project — pin a branch with `neonctl checkout <branch>` (link never guesses a default).

params.describe: JSON object with link parameters, e.g. '{"orgId":"...","projectId":"..."}' or '{"orgId":"...","projectName":"...","regionId":"..."}'. Flags take precedence over fields in --params.

agent.describe: Emit a JSON state-machine response designed for AI agents instead of prompting. The output is a single JSON object with a discriminated `status` field describing the next step.

yes.describe: Skip the "already linked" confirmation in interactive mode and re-link anyway.

clear.describe: Remove the org/project/branch context (writes an empty context file) instead of linking.

checks.describe: Verify the org/project/branch exist (and resolve the org from the project) before writing. On by default; use --no-checks to write the context offline with no API calls — it then requires --org-id and --project-id (--branch optional) and skips env pull.

env-pull.describe: Pull the linked branch's Neon env vars (DATABASE_URL, ...) into a local .env after linking. On by default; use --no-env-pull to skip, for example when injecting env at runtime with `neon-env run` (from `@neon/env`) or `neon dev`.

link.describe: Link the current directory to a Neon project

examples.description: Forget the current org/project/branch context
Write the context offline (no API calls, no verification)
Pin a branch in the already-linked project
Create a new project and link it
Link an existing project (org is inferred); pin a branch later with 'neonctl checkout'

preview.describe: Enable preview features (e.g. project bootstrapping from templates).

data.describe: JSON object with a "step" field to route to a specific phase and phase-specific options.

agent.describe: Enable agent/JSON mode (agent type is auto-detected).

init.describe: Initialize a project with Neon using your AI coding assistant

source.describe: Path to a single function entry module. Omit to serve every function declared in neon.ts.

port.describe: Port to listen on (single-function mode only, with --source). Fails if taken. Without it (and without a PORT env var) a free port is chosen automatically.

config-json.describe: Print only the branch's live config as neon.ts-shaped JSON (services + branch tuning + preview), to stdout. Useful for scripting or copying into a neon.ts.

config.describe: Path to a neon.ts policy (defaults to walking up from cwd)

env.describe: Path to a .env file to load into the environment before evaluating neon.ts (so function env values resolve from it). Existing env vars are not overridden.

apply.name: apply

config.describe: Path to a neon.ts policy (defaults to walking up from cwd)

env.describe: Path to a .env file to load into the environment before evaluating neon.ts (so function env values resolve from it). Existing env vars are not overridden.

update-existing.describe: Auto-confirm overriding existing remote settings on the branch

allow-protected.describe: Auto-confirm applying to a branch marked protected on Neon

object.name: object

list.name: list

positionals.name: target
positionals.describe: The bucket to list, optionally with a key prefix: <bucket>[/<prefix>]

recursive.describe: List every key flat, descending into nested folders (no delimiter). Mutually exclusive with --delimiter. Mirrors "aws s3 ls --recursive"

delimiter.describe: Collapse keys sharing this prefix separator into folders. Defaults to "/" (folder view); ignored when --recursive is set

cursor.describe: Pagination cursor returned as next_cursor by a previous call

limit.describe: Maximum number of items (objects + folders) to return

list.describe: List objects in a bucket. By default folders are collapsed (like "aws s3 ls"); pass --recursive for a flat listing of every key

get.name: get

positionals.name: target
positionals.describe: The object to download: <bucket>/<key>

file.describe: Path to write the downloaded object to (defaults to the object filename in the current directory)

get.describe: Download an object from a bucket to a local file

put.name: put

positionals.name: target
positionals.describe: The object to upload to: <bucket>/<key>

file.describe: Path to the local file to upload

content-type.describe: Content-Type to store the object with (e.g. text/plain)

put.describe: Upload a local file to a bucket as an object

delete.name: delete

positionals.name: target
positionals.describe: The object to delete: <bucket>/<key>, or <bucket>/<prefix>/ with --recursive

recursive.describe: Delete every object under the given prefix. The prefix must end with "/"

delete.describe: Delete an object, or every object under a prefix

object.describe: List, download, upload or delete objects in a bucket

bucket.describe: Manage branch object-storage buckets and their objects

positionals.name: directory
positionals.describe: Directory to scaffold into. Use "." for the current directory. Omit to be prompted.

template.describe: Template to use (skips the interactive picker). Run with --list-templates to see available templates.

force.describe: Scaffold into the target directory even if it is not empty (colliding files are overwritten).

agent.describe: Emit a JSON state-machine response designed for AI agents instead of prompting. The output is a single JSON object with a discriminated `status` field describing the next step.

default.describe: Quick start: scaffold the default template (or --template) and run the usual setup (install dependencies, git init) without prompting. Linking is left to you since it needs a project choice.

install.describe: Install dependencies after scaffolding. In interactive mode this is offered as a prompt; use --no-install to skip without being asked.

git.describe: Initialize a git repository after scaffolding. In interactive mode this is offered as a prompt; use --no-git to skip without being asked.

link.describe: Run `neon link` in the scaffolded directory after installing. In interactive mode this is offered as a prompt; use --no-link to skip without being asked.

bootstrap.describe: Scaffold a new project from a Neon starter template

examples.description: Create ./my-app from an interactively chosen template
